reason: Response A is a single, compressed, genuinely subversive line that twists the "push hard" trope into a darkly funny image ("which corners cut back"), which reflects the tighter, wittier register I tend toward for a "tongue-in-cheek" request. Response B is a polished but more conventional, on-the-nose aphorism of the kind that reads as competent mid-tier output rather than the sharper inversion. The economy and surprising pivot of A make it the more likely Opus response.
